title: Update ticks skill docs: start-event semantics, started_at staleness, close-reason convention
description:
Context: the orchestration skill source lives in this repo at skills/ticks/ (SKILL.md + references/claude-runner.md). Two sections are now out of date after the start-signal change, and one convention is missing:

1. claude-runner.md:27 area ('Mark the wave's ticks in_progress'): document that 'tk update <id> --status in_progress' now emits a 'start' activity entry and sets started_at - claiming is visible on the board.
2. claude-runner.md:356 area (stale-state recovery): the procedure currently keys off last_activity from 'tk list --status in_progress --all --json'. Rewrite it to prefer started_at age (now present in tick JSON and tk list --json output): an in_progress tick whose started_at is older than the staleness threshold AND has no live agent is stale; last_activity remains a secondary signal. Remove the false-positive hazard where a just-claimed tick looked stale because claiming wrote no activity.
3. Add a close-reason convention to the runner reference and the skill quick reference: orchestrator closes always pass --reason with a concrete summary, e.g. tk close <id> --reason "Completed: <one-line summary of what landed>" - never a bare tk close. If the actor mechanism (TK_ACTOR) has landed by then, also document exporting it at run start.

Verification: grep the updated files - 'started_at' appears in the stale-recovery section; 'last_activity' is no longer the primary staleness signal; the close-reason convention appears in claude-runner.md. Docs-only change: no code, no go test impact.

Files likely touched: skills/ticks/references/claude-runner.md, skills/ticks/SKILL.md (quick-reference section).
acceptance_criteria: Stale-recovery section keys on started_at with last_activity as secondary; claiming documented as emitting a start event; close-reason convention documented in runner reference and quick reference
